candidates.title: MGS MARS ORBITER LASER ALTIMETER PEDR AND EGDR ARCHIVES (MEGDR)
candidates.reasoning: Regional topography to evaluate deposit setting, basin slopes, potential paleodrainage and catchments across Arabia Terra—context for distinguishing depositional systems (e.g., valley networks vs. basin fills).

candidates.title: MARS EXPRESS HRSC DATA (REFDR DTM)
candidates.reasoning: Higher-resolution DTMs than MOLA in many areas; useful for morphometrics of putative fluvial deposits (channel gradients, fan/delta surfaces, inverted channels) to test for multiple fluvial styles.

CRISM multispectral RDRs support compositional discrimination (e.g., phyllosilicates/sulfates/iron oxides) that can separate depositional environments (lacustrine vs. fluvial channel/fan vs. altered basement) within Arabia Terra.
CRISM targeted RDRs (higher spectral sampling) for detailed mineralogy of specific deposits; helps test whether deposits represent a single system or multiple episodes/environments.

tool_input.summary: No GEO-node dataset is indexed specifically as 'Arabia Terra deposits' or 'fluvial style'. The most relevant GEO holdings to address the question are the regional/topographic and compositional datasets commonly used to map and interpret depositional systems in Arabia Terra: MGS MOLA MEGDR (inspected; dataset_id confirmed), MEX HRSC DTMs (inspected; dataset_id confirmed), and MRO CRISM RDR products (paths verified by browsing the MRO directory). THEMIS/CTX/HiRISE imaging products are not surfaced as canonical dataset IDs in the GEO holdings index in the tool environment, so they are not listed here.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS Geosciences node at https://pds-geosciences.wustl.edu/.

Tools:
  - pds_geo_search_holdings(query): fuzzy-search the GEO node's published index of every archive on the site. The index contains canonical dataset IDs (e.g. `MEX-M-HRSC-5-REFDR-DTM-V1.0`, `urn:nasa:pds:mer2_pancam_sci_calibrated2`) — not science topics or geographic names. Search using mission abbreviations and instrument names. Returns canonical dataset IDs with a `mission_hint`.
  - pds_geo_browse(path): list sub-directories and files at a path on the GEO node. Returns names and URLs.
  - pds_geo_inspect_with_collections(path): inspect a path and, when a PDS4 bundle is present, also fetch its collection labels one level deeper. Returns `labels` (bundle/voldesc) plus `collections` (PDS4 collections only). For PDS3 volumes, returns voldesc labels and `collections` is empty.

Directory layout:
  mission/ → dataset_or_bundle/ → volume/ (PDS3) or sub-collections (PDS4)

Top-level mission directories: m2020, insight, msl, mro, mer, mex, ody, phx, mgs, mpf, viking, mariner, mars, mgn, premgn, venus, messenger, grail, clps, lunar, lro, earth, lab, near.

Common mission/instrument abbreviations:
  Mars Express = MEX (instruments: HRSC, OMEGA, MARSIS, PFS, SPICAM, MaRS)
  Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter = MRO (instruments: HiRISE, CTX, CRISM, SHARAD, MCS)
  Mars Science Laboratory / Curiosity = MSL (instruments: ChemCam, APXS, CheMin, SAM, Mastcam, MAHLI, DAN)
  Mars 2020 / Perseverance = M2020 (instruments: PIXL, SHERLOC, Mastcam-Z, SuperCam, RIMFAX)
  Mars Exploration Rovers (Spirit=MER2, Opportunity=MER1) (instruments: Pancam, Mini-TES/MTES, APXS, MB, MI)
  Mars Global Surveyor = MGS (instruments: MOC, MOLA, TES, MAG)
  Mars Odyssey = ODY (instruments: THEMIS, GRS, NS)
  Phoenix = PHX (instruments: TEGA, MECA, SSI, OM, RAC)
  Viking = VL1/VL2/VO1/VO2 (instruments: camera, IRTM, MAWD)
  MESSENGER = MESS (instruments: MDIS, GRNS, XRS, MLA, MASCS)
  Magellan = MGN (instruments: SAR, altimetry, radiometry)
  LRO (instruments: LOLA, Diviner, LROC, Mini-RF, LAMP)
  GRAIL (instruments: LGRS)
  NEAR (instruments: NLR, MSI, XGRS, MAG)

PDS3 vs PDS4: both formats co-exist on the same node. PDS3 directory names are ALL-CAPS-style identifiers; PDS4 bundle directories begin with `urn-nasa-pds-`.

Bundle vs collection (PDS4): a bundle directory holds the bundle label plus sub-directories containing collection labels. `inspect_with_collections` returns both levels.

Extracting dataset_id from labels:
  - PDS3: VOLUME.DATA_SET_ID
  - PDS4 bundle: Identification_Area.logical_identifier (in `labels`)
  - PDS4 collection: Identification_Area.logical_identifier (in `collections`)

Approach:
  - Queries are often science-level questions from published papers. Before searching, infer which mission(s) and instrument(s) are relevant using the abbreviation table above, then search holdings with those terms.
  - If holdings search returns nothing useful, browse the relevant mission directory (e.g. `mro/`) and inspect promising datasets from there.

Constraints:
  - Stay under ~15 tool calls per query.
  - Only return paths you've actually inspected — never guess.
  - If nothing matches, return an empty candidate list with a clear summary.
Do the Arabia Terra deposits record only one fluvial style or a diverse range of depositional systems?
